How to brew espresso at home

By Jane Doe — May 4, 2026

Brewing espresso at home is not as hard as it sounds. Start with fresh, properly-roasted beans and grind them just before use. Aim for a fine, even grind — finer than drip coffee but coarser than Turkish coffee.

Pull a shot in roughly 25 to 30 seconds. The crema should be a warm, hazelnut brown. If it pulls too fast, the grind is too coarse. Too slow, and your grind is too fine or your dose is too high.

Practice makes perfect. A good shot of espresso has balance: sweetness, acidity, and bitterness in harmony. Don't be afraid to dial in for a week before you get it right.
